item.dc:creator: sarukane

item.title: Band of Brothers biggest error.
item.description:
Albert Blithe was the main character for the third episode of the HBO series "Band of Brothers". It was believed that he passed from his wounds since E Company did not know what happened to him. After the shows release, the family of Blithe announced to the publisher that this wasn't true.
After receiving the Purple Heart Blithe actually recovered from his wound but was discharged from the war. After his discharge he found himself a job at Westinghouse Electrics, he then later re-enlisted in 1949 and 1954 where he served with the 187th airborne. He later married a woman named Kay and had 2 kids, a son and a daughter. On 1967 he died of a perforated ulcer after a trip to commemorate the Battle of Bulge. I thought I should Post this so he could get the recognition he deserves since his life was only shown in the republished version of the novel. TLDR: Blithe didn't die in war and got married and had kids. Still a great show though!
item.dc:creator: barkmank1

item.title: Well, now it has a name, at least
item.description: How do I make it go away? Thanks to most for the kind words. Thanks to the rest to remind me not to take myself too seriously. I stay active, professionally and with significant amounts of volunteer work. It's the quiet times that are so empty. ADHD for sure, medication is a miracle and I'd be a real basket case without it. Take your meds, people!

run it by me again
dang

seems easy enough
fewer?
just hit OK
i guess
now i want a goldfish sandwich
it is an outfit
sorry gang
acccurate
that's amazing actually
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!

item.title: Fun facts about Gen X
item.description:
As a Gen Xer, these are some interesting facts about our generation
Generation X is anyone born between 1965 and 1980. This makes the parents of most Gen Xers either the silent generation or boomers. Generation X was also the first generation named simply after a letter of the alphabet.
Gen Xers witnessed the invention of personal computers.
People born into Gen X are sometimes known as the “sandwich generation.” We often have to simultaneously financially support both our aging parents and our children.
ATMs were invented during this generation.
We’re also called the “MTV Generation.” Many an hour was spent watching MTV after school.
People born in this generation tend to have higher lead exposure. Lead use peaked around the 1960s and 1970s, though, when early Gen Xers were still kids.
We’re also typically very independent due to the fact that many of us were “latch-key” kids. Many of our parents weren’t home after school due to work and we had to be self-reliant.
We experienced a second wave of feminism, around the 1960s to 1980s, when most Gen Xers were teenagers. One of the defining ideas of the second wave was that women were tired of being stuck in the traditional roles of wives and mothers, looking after the house and children. The end result was increases in women’s pay and increased rights of reproductive freedom. thefactsite.com
item.dc:creator: sistermoon

item.title: 3rd birthday in a row where only websites I've signed up for remember. Happy birthday... to me...
item.description: I turned 41 this year. I was laid off last month. I've yet to find a new job or get an interview. Today tops the all time shit birthdays I've had. *sigh*

item.title: A semi-random post of tumblr and other memes with slightly more than usual informative memes so that I can get the trophy. Also a title that's ridiculously long to make an annoyingly long URL
item.description:
They're not as common as this post implies and not all of them become more visible under UV light and cats can't see all of those frequencies. Someone summarizing the research said it like this: "So basically a small subset of humans with specific genetic conditions have lines of Blaschko and small portion of these lines are best visualized under UV light. I suspect CSI’s dramatization of this turned it into a generalization because the early the articles popped up around that time. "
the link: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Harry_Allen_(trans_man)

item.dc:creator: ChampRickets

item.title: I might have a problem
item.description: I eat them like candy I buy 12 jars a month and each jar is 32oz. Factoring in the weight of the brine (which I also drink most of), that's about 1½ lbs of jalapenos per jar, which means I eat about 18lbs of jalapenos a month. So if all the calculations are correct, that means I've had about 99lbs of jalapenos this year so far. I hope you've enjoyed my useless trivia as much as I enjoy jalapenos. If no one has told you enough recently, I love you and am proud of all you've done, thank you for being here. And as always, Shine Brightly, Beautiful People ♡ Viral edit: To hopefully quell some of your concerns regarding my health: One of my daily pills I take for HRT is a diuretic and I didn't have this habit prior to starting my transition. Prior to them, I still had low sodium levels, so I have a LOT of leniency and this is the way I get most of my salt.
item.dc:creator: MatronOfMorgues

item.title: Never too old to learn something new
item.description: I was raised conservative evangelical Christian and home schooled up until college. It took a long time to un-learn the falsehoods that were ingrained in me. I'm still learning new things and will continue to learn. My life has been a journey and it's only halfway through (hopefully). Happy Pride!

item.title: Sinking Of Amoco Cadiz In Brittany 1978 Looks Like A Giant Whale Surfacing.
item.description: Operating under the Liberian flag, she ran aground on 16 March 1978 on Portsall Rocks, 2 km (1.2 mi) from the coast of Brittany, France. Ultimately she split in three and sank, resulting in the largest oil spill of its kind in history to that date.
item.dc:creator: iceage2012
